channel.title: Floods on Iporã Brito Possantti
channel.description: Recent content in Floods on Iporã Brito Possantti

item.title: I think some water's coming down
item.description:
Cinematic reality

I think some water’s coming down, eh

That is what I wrote on 29 April 2024, a year ago, in a WhatsApp group of professors and students from the Institute of Hydraulic Research (IPH). The group had been created in September 2023, in the context of the heavy floods that inundated cities in the Taquari River Valley.

This group became active again in November 2023, when other rains caused new floods, this time hitting Porto Alegre and the metropolitan region. On that occasion, I helped create material for the press, which was released to illustrate what would happen if a flood like the one in 1941 were to occur again, without the dyke protection system. It was featured in G1 and Correio do Povo.

item.title: A great project
item.description:
Hydrology | Standard Methods
A very interesting project

Abstract: This project was a complex problem in catchment hydrology. Earum laudantium iste voluptates rerum placeat ipsum. Asperiores ducimus dolores rerum et dolor possimus sit nihil. Nam quae ex magnam. Vel minima animi quisquam sit.

Ultimate client: Water Agency
Intermediate: The Nice NGO
My role: Hydrologist
Tools applied: Python, QGIS, SQL

Graphical Abstract

Context

The project addressed hydrological behavior in a catchment with recurring flood issues and limited prior analysis.
